description: Known agent skill registries and sources. Used by extract-skill-provenance.js to resolve missing skill metadata. Each registry entry describes how to search it, what data it returns, and how trustworthy its results are.

registries.name: Skyll
registries.description: REST API and MCP server that aggregates skills from multiple sources (skills.sh, curated registry, and more) in real time. Supports keyword search and returns full SKILL.md content with a relevance score (0–100). The best single API to try first because it fans out across sources automatically.

searchStrategy.notes: No API key required. Rate-limited to reasonable use. Results ranked by relevance + popularity.

registries.name: Tessl Registry
registries.description: Curated skill registry with Snyk security scanning on every skill. Each skill has a quality score, security score, and impact score alongside standard metadata. Skills are organized by GitHub owner/repo/skill-name. Partnered with Snyk for supply-chain security — the most trustworthy third-party registry.

searchStrategy.notes: Direct URL works well when you know the owner/repo. Skill metadata is embedded in the page. No API key needed.

registries.name: SkillsMP
registries.description: Searchable marketplace of 13,000+ Claude Code skills indexed from GitHub. Supports keyword search and AI semantic search via REST API. Free tier allows 50 requests/day without an API key (keyword search only). Authenticated users get 500/day plus AI search.

searchStrategy.notes: No API key required for basic keyword search. Returns GitHub repo URLs as source. Rate limit: 50 req/day anonymous, 10 req/min.

registries.name: Awesome GitHub Copilot
registries.description: GitHub's official community collection of skills for Copilot, Claude Code, and other agents. Maintained by GitHub with community contributions. Machine-readable llms.txt available. Skills installable via `gh skills install github/awesome-copilot <skill-name>`. One of the largest curated sources (~600+ resources).

searchStrategy.notes: Check if the skill exists by fetching the raw SKILL.md. If 200, parse frontmatter for metadata. The llms.txt index lists all skills in a machine-readable format.

registries.name: obra/superpowers
registries.description: Jesse Vincent's canonical agentic skills framework. The authoritative source for brainstorming, writing-plans, executing-plans, systematic-debugging, test-driven-development, subagent-driven-development, code-review, using-git-worktrees, and verification-before-completion skills. Used by Claude Code, Codex, Copilot CLI, Cursor, Gemini CLI, and OpenCode. License: MIT.
registries.author: Jesse Vincent (obra)

searchStrategy.notes: Check knownSkills list first before fetching. The skill name in the lock file may have a 'superpowers-' prefix that should be stripped before lookup (e.g. superpowers-writing-plans → writing-plans).

registries.name: anthropics/skills
registries.description: Anthropic's official skills repository. Powers Claude.ai's built-in file creation capabilities. Contains production-grade skills for docx, pptx, xlsx, pdf, frontend-design, algorithmic-art, and more. All skills are MIT licensed and maintained by Anthropic engineers.
registries.author: Anthropic

searchStrategy.notes: Check knownSkills list first. Skills are at the repo root level, not in a skills/ subdirectory.

registries.name: skills.rest
registries.description: Large aggregator marketplace with 430,000+ vetted agent skills for OpenClaw, Claude, and Codex. Skills are accessible via direct URL pattern. The source type 'skills-rest' in skills-lock.json refers to this registry. Snyk-authored and mahipal-authored skills (snyk-fix, scanning-docker-images-with-trivy) are known to come from here.

searchStrategy.notes: Direct URL lookup by skill name. Site returns 403 to automated requests in some configurations — treat failures gracefully. Known skills list above can be used as a fast local check before making a network request.

registries.name: LobeHub Skills Marketplace
registries.description: Skills marketplace with previews, install prompts, and metadata for skills across Claude Code, OpenClaw, and other agents. Good for discovering security and DevOps skills. Includes the Snyk obra/superpowers adapter (obra-using-superpowers) and Trivy container scanner skills.

searchStrategy.notes: skill IDs use author-repo-skillname format e.g. 'faulkdev-github-copilot-superpowers-obra-using-superpowers'. Better used for manual lookup than automated search — skill ID cannot be derived from skill name alone.

registries.name: github/awesome-copilot (GitHub API)
registries.description: The underlying GitHub repository for the Awesome Copilot collection. Can be queried via GitHub API to list all skill directory names. Useful as a fallback existence check — if a skill name matches a directory in this repo, the source is github/awesome-copilot with MIT license.

searchStrategy.notes: The API returns a directory listing. Extract 'name' fields to get all skill names. Then do a direct raw fetch if you need the SKILL.md content. No auth needed for public repo but GitHub rate-limits unauthenticated requests to 60/hour. Set GITHUB_TOKEN env var to raise to 5000/hour.
